text:
The DCE iron ore futures continued to hit boƩom today, with the most-traded I2609 contract closing at 739.5 yuan/mt,down 0.87%. Port spot prices fell by 3-5 yuan/mt from pre-holiday levels. Traders showed low selling enthusiasm, with many holding off from offering. Wait-and-see sentiment intensified among steel mills, and only a few purchased as needed. The overall trading atmosphere was sluggish. As of now, very few deals were concluded. Last week, SMM total global iron ore shipments reached 32.98 million mt, down 7.62% WoW, while cumulative shipments were up 1.9% YoY. Shipments from both Australia and Brazil declined, while those from non-mainstream countries edged up. In addition, SMM total China iron ore arrivals stood at 27.81 million mt last week, down 7.14% WoW, with cumulative growth of 5.85% YoY. This week, supply pressure eased slightly. However, as steel mill losses deepened and blast furnace maintenance increased, hot metal output is expected to decline this week. Iron ore demand weakened, leaving ore prices under downward pressure.

Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today, DCE iron ore futures opened weak in the morning session and saw a modest recovery in the afternoon,with the most-traded I2609 contract closing at 738.5 yuan/mt, down 0.54% from the previous trading session. Port spot prices held steady from the previous day. Traders mostly followed market trends, with moderate quoting enthusiasm. Steel mills made fewer inquiries, and spot trading volume was moderate as of the time of writing. This week, the SMM survey showed the impact from blast furnace maintenance was 1.1633 million mt, up 31,600 mt WoW. Iron ore demand weakened, and hot metal may pull back due to maintenance and environmental protection-driven production restrictions. In addition, expectations for the ninth round of coke price increases fermented. Weak sentiment in coal and coke spilled over to iron ore, coupled with falling oil prices and interest rate hike expectations weighing on overall sentiment in the ferrous metals market. Iron ore prices are expected to remain in the doldrums in the short term.
Linked asset: 23232.jpg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today, DCE iron ore futures strengthened in the morning session and dipped slightly in the afternoon today,with the most-traded I2609 contract closing at 744 yuan/mt, up 0.74% from the previous trading session. Port spot prices rose 4-9 yuan/mt from the prior day. Miner activity was moderate, with steel mills primarily purchasing for essential restocking; spot trading volume has been mediocre thus far. On the fundamentals front, according to an SMM survey, the daily average hot metal output of sampled steel mills this week was 2.4607 million mt, down 4,300 mt WoW. Pig iron production has reached an inflection point, and iron ore demand is expected to weaken going forward. On the news front, market rumors of tightening iron ore supply disrupted the near-term bearish sentiment. Taking all factors into account, iron ore prices are likely to move sideways in the short term, while the medium and long-term outlook remains bearish.
Linked asset: 24062026chinese23.jpg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today, DCE iron ore futures weakened, with the most-traded contract I2609 closing at 735 yuan/mt,down 1.08% from the previous trading session. Port spot prices fell 5-7 yuan/mt from the previous day. Traders showed modest willingness to quote, while steel mills had a strong desire to push for lower prices. As of now, spot trading volume was relatively low. Looking ahead, amid the steel off-season, export demand for finished steel continued to weaken, driving pig iron production to a turning point, and iron ore demand is expected to shrink further. Additionally, rumors of environmental protection-driven production restrictions, combined with weakening downstream export data, triggered market pessimism, leaving ore prices more likely to fall than rise. In the short term, iron ore prices may trend sideways in a tug-of-war between longs and shorts, while the medium- and long-term outlook is persistently bearish.
Linked asset: 56-1.jpg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today, the DCE iron ore futures trended strong today, pulling back slightly near the close. The most-traded I2609contract closed at 748 yuan/ mt, up 0.81% from the previous trading session. Port spot prices rose 8-11 yuan/mt from the prior day. Trader quoting activity was moderate, steel mills maintained a strong wait-and-see stance, and spot trading volumes were moderate as of now. Looking ahead, iron ore port data for this period, according to an SMM survey, showed a slight destocking. Total inventory reached 148.66 million mt, down 690,000 mt MoM, while port pick-up volume edged down 38,000 mt to 3.23 million mt. The destocking was mainly driven by a decline in port arrivals. Overall, the iron ore supply side remained loose. On the news front, market rumors were frequent, and the tug-of-war between longs and shorts was fierce, with no clear divergence yet. With fundamentals and sentiment intertwined, ore prices are likely to continue moving sideways in the short term.
Linked asset: 26062026chinese567.png
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today on the DCE iron ore futures first weakened and then strengthened, with contract I2609 finally closing at 746 yuan/ton,up 0.67% from the previous trading session. Port spot prices were unchanged from the previous trading day. Trader activity was moderate, and steel mills purchased on an as-needed basis, with spot transaction volumes remaining lackluster so far. In the short term, iron ore supply continues to ease. According to SMM statistics, China's iron ore arrivals reached 29.33 million tonnes last week, up 5.47% month-on-month and 5.94% year-on-year. Meanwhile, the latest SMM data shows that total inventory across 35 ports stood at approximately 148 million tonnes, with destocking beginning to slow down. As downstream demand softens, mounting supply-side pressure on iron ore continues to cap upside price potential. At the same time, a mix of bullish and bearish rumours on the news front may drive modest upward price probes in the near term. Taken together, the market is likely to maintain a range-bound oscillatory pattern over the short term.
Linked asset: 29062026chinese23.jpg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today, the iron ore market on the Dalian Commodity Exchange showed a volatile trading pattern,with contract I2609 eventually closing at 747 yuan/ton, up +0.61% compared with the previous trading session. Port spot prices rose by 0-3 yuan/ton from the previous day. Trader activity was moderate, while steel mill purchases remained largely needôbased; as of now, spot market transaction volumes have been average. The current iron ore fundamentals continue to show a bearish bias. Supply remains loose, with no clear sign of reversal, while demand has entered a plateau and is expected to continue its downward trend in the future. This week's SMM survey data indicate that blast furnace maintenance resulted in hot metal output impact of 1,156,300 tonnes, down by 48,700 tonnes weekôonôweek, with the projected impact for next week reaching 1,200,500 tonnes. On the news front, most factors are marginal disturbances, with bullish news taking the lead but not yet pointing to a directional trend. Therefore, considering all factors, shortôterm iron ore prices are expected to maintain a rangeôbound consolidation pattern. Linked asset: 30062026chinesew.jpg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
The Dalian Commodity Exchange iron ore market showed volatile trading today, with contract I2609 finally closing at 733 yuan/ton,down 1.68% from the previous trading session. Port spot prices fell by 10-12 yuan/ton from the prior day. Trader activity was moderate, while steel mill inquiries increased; however, spot transaction volumes remained generally modest as of now. Iron ore demand is expected to continue declining in July. On the one hand, steel is affected by the off-season weather, with both domestic demand and exports declining, putting downward pressure on prices. At the same time, the ninth round of coke price hikes has been implemented, and expectations for a tenth round remain strong, further compressing steel mill profits and thus heightening expectations of production cuts. According to this week's SMM survey, sample steel mill output stood at 2.456 million tons, down 4,700 tons from the previous week, and some mills have already begun scheduling maintenance plans, suggesting that hot metal output will likely decline further. In summary, the short-term outlook for iron ore prices is bearish.
Linked asset: 01072026china.jpg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today's DCE iron ore futures showed weakness after rallying in the night session.Contract I2609 settled at 740 yuan/ton, up 0.48% from the previous trading day. Port spot prices rose 5-8 yuan/ton versus the prior session. Trader activity was moderate, with steel mills procuring on an as-needed basis; spot trading volumes have remained unremarkable so far.
The iron ore supply-demand fundamentals remain largely stable, with supply on the loose side. Going forward, the pace of mine shipments is expected to slow, keeping the overall picture relatively steady. On the demand side, Southeast Asian steel exports have entered the oﬀ-season, prompting regional traders to actively cut prices to compete for market share. As a result, domestic export demand has begun to soften. However, domestic steel inventories still have buﬀer room, and this is unlikely to trigger production-cut intentions among steel mills in the near term. On the news front, aside from supply-tightening rumors for speciﬁc varieties, there are no events decisive enough to drive market direction.
Taken together, iron ore prices are expected to trade in a range-bound pattern in the short term.

Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today, iron ore futures on the DCE traded weaker, with contract 12609 finally closing at 734 yuan/ton,down 1.74% from the previous trading day. Port spot prices fell by 3-8 yuan/ton from the prior day. Trader activity was moderate, while steel mill purchases were mainly for replenishment; spot market transaction volumes have been average so far.
According to SMM surveys, iron ore inventory at 35 major ports nationwide this week stood at 148.30 million tons, down 36,000 tons week-on-week, indicating a slight overall destocking. Meanwhile, average daily outbound shipments from ports rose by 68,000 tons to 3.298 million tons, reflecting relatively sound short-term demand. Next week, arriving shipments are expected to increase further, tilting resource supply toward looser conditions; on the demand side, as hot metal output gradually declines, outbound shipment volumes are likely to follow suit. Support for iron ore prices is weakening, but n the near term, prices are likely to fluctuate within a range, as supply and demand dynamics remain largely in line with market expectations.

Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today, DCE iron ore futures trended strong but pulled back slightly in the afternoon.Contract 12609 closed at 738 yuan/mt, up 0.14% from the previous trading day. Port spot prices rose 2-3 yuan/mt from the previous day. Traders were active in quoting, steel mills purchased as needed, inquiry interest was moderate, with spot transaction volumes remaining low
Looking ahead, as the downstream enters the off-season and the pace of mine shipments slows, the supply side of iron ore is expected to tighten slightly, though the overall loose pattern will be hard to change. Last week, SMM's global iron ore shipments totaled 35.16 million mt, up 1.53% WoW, with cumulative YoY growth of 1.77%. Among them, Australia's shipments edged down 6% WoW, while Brazil's surged nearly 20% WoW, and non-mainstream countries' shipments edged up slightly WoW. Among the four major miners, shipments from Rio Tinto and FMG declined. In addition, last week, SMM's China iron ore arrivals totaled 28.78 million mt, edging down 1.88% WoW, with cumulative YoY growth of 6.07%. Overall, iron ore prices are likely to fluctuate within a range in the short term, while the long-term outlook remains bearish.
Linked asset: 672025hb.jpeg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today, DCE iron ore futures trended weak, with the most-traded contract I2609 eventuallyclosing at 759 yuan/mt, down 0.78% from the previous trading day. Port spot prices fell 5 yuan/mt from the prior day. Traders acted in line with the market, with moderate quoting activity; steel mills mainly made need-based restocking purchases, with few inquiries; as of now, spot transaction volumes were moderate. Shipping data shows that China's iron ore supply has entered a loose phase, and iron ore prices will gradually come under pressure. According to SMMdata, total global iron ore shipment volume last week fell 3.88% WoW to 33.8317 million mt, and total iron ore arrivals into China rose 2% WoW to 27.54 million mt. Meanwhile, the decline in port pick-up volumes also indicates that steel mills' demand for iron ore is starting to slow down. With coking coal and coke price rise expectations still in place, even if hot metal prices remain high, steel mills' acceptance of iron ore prices is relatively tight, making ore prices overall more likely to fall than rise. In the short term, iron ore prices may continue to be under pressure, showing a weak trend overall.
Linked asset: 08062026chinee3.jpg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today, DCE iron ore futures trended weak, with the most-traded contract I2609 eventuallyclosing at 759 yuan/mt, down 0.78% from the previous trading day. Port spot prices fell 5 yuan/mt from the prior day. Traders acted in line with the market, with moderate quoting activity; steel mills mainly made need-based restocking purchases, with few inquiries; as of now, spot transaction volumes were moderate. Shipping data shows that China's iron ore supply has entered a loose phase, and iron ore prices will gradually come under pressure. According to SMMdata, total global iron ore shipment volume last week fell 3.88% WoW to 33.8317 million mt, and total iron ore arrivals into China rose 2% WoW to 27.54 million mt. Meanwhile, the decline in port pick-up volumes also indicates that steel mills' demand for iron ore is starting to slow down. With coking coal and coke price rise expectations still in place, even if hot metal prices remain high, steel mills' acceptance of iron ore prices is relatively tight, making ore prices overall more likely to fall than rise. In the short term, iron ore prices may continue to be under pressure, showing a weak trend overall.

Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

Jun 8th, 2026 CFR Qingdao, USD/dry tonne Today, DCE iron ore futures trended weak, with the most-traded contract I2609 eventually closing at 759 yuan/mt, down 0.78% from the previous
trading day. Port spot prices fell 5 yuan/mt from the prior day. Traders acted in line with the market, with moderate quo(cid:415)ng ac(cid:415)vity; steel mills mainly
made need-based restocking purchases, with few inquiries; as of now, spot transac(cid:415)on volumes were moderate.

IRON ORE DOMESTIC CONCENTRATE INDEX CALCULATION METHODOLOGY
The compila(cid:415)on method for price index generally refers to the compila(cid:415)on method of CPI price index and other price indices, breakdown the price data and calculate the average value according
to a certain method, taking the ver(cid:415)cal axis as the regional composite index (average of different grade index) and the horizontal axis as the grade composite index (average of different regional
index), a total composite index for domes(cid:415)c ore can be output ul(cid:415)mately. The process system is also adopted in the calcula(cid:415)on i.e. each sub-index can be obtained as well.

text:
Today, the DCE iron ore futures trended weaker today, with contract 12609 closing at 735.5 yuan/mt,down 0.47% from the previous trading day. Port spot prices fell about 1-2 yuan/mt from the previous trading day. Traders were moderately active in offering, and steel mills mostly restocked as needed.
As of now, spot transaction volumes were moderate.
Iron ore demand is expected to continue declining this week. According to SMM statistics, the hot metal impact from blast furnace maintenance between July 4 and July 10 was 1.2637 million mt, up 77,800 mt WoW, and the impact is expected to increase by another 64,500 mt next week to 1.3282 million mt. In the short term, iron ore demand is expected to keep contracting, price support will gradually weaken, and overall ore prices may fluctuate within a range in a bearish trend. Linked asset: 77202589.jpeg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today, DCE iron ore futures trended weakly, with contract 12609 closing at 746 yuan/mt, up 0.88% from the previous trading day.Port spot prices rose by about 5-7 yuan/mt from the previous day. Trader activity was average, and steel mills purchased on an as-needed basis. As of now, spot trading sentiment been
The latest SMM survey data further confirmed the weakening trend in iron ore demand. Statistics show that the national blast furnace operating rate fell to 89.78%, and daily average pig iron production dropped to 2.4406 million mt, down 15,400 mt MoM. Additionally, affected by commemorative events in Tangshan, local environmental protection-related maintenance is expected to intensify, and the weakening trend in hot metal output is likely to strengthen further. In the short term, the bearish fundamental backdrop has deepened. However, given the currently high proportion of short positions in the futures market, selling pressure is nearly exhausted, and the downward momentum for prices has begun to fade. Short-term ore prices are likely to consolidate and move sideways. Going forward, attention should be paid to whether a reduction in short positions could drive a price rebound.

Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today, the DCE iron ore futures trended weak today, with contract 12609 closing at 745.5 yuan/mt, up 0.27% from the previous trading day.Port spot prices ranged from -2 to +3 yuan/mt compared with the previous trading day. Trader activity was moderate, steel mills made few inquiries, and of now, spot transaction volumes were low.
According to the SMM survey, total inventory at the 10 main ports stood at 108.14 million mt, down 1.32 million mt MoM. Among them, fines destocking slowed, lump ore destocking was good, and pellet inventory was at an extremely low level. In the short term, pellet and lump premiums are expected to remain firm, while fines prices will find it difficult to break higher as inventory accumulates. Demand side, steel mills' purchase willingness showed a weakening trend MoM. In the short term, iron ore prices are expected to continue fluctuating with limited upside and resistance to downward pressure on prices.
Linked asset: 972022640.jpeg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today, the DCE iron ore futures trended weaker today, with contract 12609 closing at 751.5 yuan/mt, up 0.87% from the previous trading day.Port spot prices rose 2-5 yuan/mt from the prior trading day. Trader activity was moderate, and steel mills mainly purchased for essential restocking. As of now spot trading volumes were moderate.
SMM's latest statistics showed that total iron ore inventory at 35 main ports nationwide was 147.16 million mt, down 1.14 million mt WoW. Over the same period, the daily average port pick-up volume edged down by 23,000 mt to 3.275 million mt. This week, affected by typhoons and persistent rainy weather, port operations were disrupted, vessel berthing and unloading efficiency dropped significantly, and port congestion intensified. On the demand side, as hot metal production of steel mill blast fumaces continued to decline, the daily average port pick-up volume pulled back slightly. With increasing blast furnace maintenance at steel mills, hot metal production is still expected to decline further. Therefore, from a fundamental perspective, short-term support for iron ore prices is expected to continue weakening.
Linked asset: 107202520.jpeg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today, iron ore prices on the Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) came under pressure, with the main contract I2609 finally settling at 744.5 yuan/ton,down approximately 0.47% from the previous session. Port spot prices fell by about 4-6 yuan/ton from the previous trading day. Traders' quotation enthusiasm weakened, and steel mills' purchases were mostly for replenishment needs. As of now, spot transaction volumes have been relatively low. As of last Friday, global iron ore total shipments reached 30.381 million tons, a decrease of 14% month-on-month. Among them, shipments from both Australia and Brazil saw significant reductions. During the same period, iron ore arrivals at Chinese ports were 25.015 million tons, down 13% month-on-month. The supply side of iron ore has tightened in the short term, but given the high port inventory level (147 million tons), the overall supply side remains relatively loose. As pig iron production enters a downward trend, iron ore demand is expected to weaken further. Therefore, from a fundamental perspective, iron ore prices are likely to fluctuate within a narrow range this week, with a tendency to decline.
Linked asset: 13072026chinese.jpg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today, the iron ore market showed relatively strong performance on the Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE), with the benchmark contractI2609 finally closing at 760.5 yuan/ton, up 1.81%. Qingdao port spot prices remained firm, rising by approximately 10-12 yuan/ton from the previous trading day. Traders became more active, while steel mills, apart from rigid replenishment, showed generally low purchasing willingness. Spot transaction volumes have remained modest so far. In terms of mill maintenance schedules, iron ore demand is expected to decline further. According to SMM statistics, the blast furnace maintenance-induced hot metal impact for this week (July 11-July 17) is 1.3434 million tons, an increase of 70,100 tons from the previous week. For next week (July 18-July 24), the impact is projected to reach 1.4109 million tons, up by 67,500 tons week-on-week. On the news front, the strike at Port Hedland and the upcoming shipping restrictions are likely to tighten supply side in the short term. Taken together, iron ore prices may see a modest bottom rebound in the near term, but are still expected to remain within a range-bound fluctuation pattern overall.
Linked asset: 14072026chinese34.jpg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today, the iron ore market showed strong performance on the Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE), with the benchmark contract I2609ultimately closing at 762 RMB/ton, up 1.13%. At Qingdao port, spot prices edged up by about 0-2 RMB/ton from the previous trading day, as trading volumes remained thin. Traders' offer enthusiasm was generally moderate, while steel mills showed weak purchasing interest. Spot transactions so far today have been limited. SMM survey data confirm continued weakness in iron ore demand. According to the survey results, the blast furnace operating rate across 242 Chinese steel mills this week stood at 89.79%, and the average daily hot metal output of sampled mills reached 2.4382 million tons, down 2,400 tons from the previous week. The fundamental support for iron ore prices continues to weaken. On the news front, some mining firms are reportedly set to implement vessel loading restrictions at ports as early as tomorrow, sparking intense market sentiment that drove up futures prices. However, such factors are unlikely to provide sustained support for iron ore prices over the longer term. Considering supply-demand dynamics and sentiment, iron ore prices are likely to remain firm in the short term until sentiment fades and prices revert to fundamental drivers.
Linked asset: 15072026chinese234.jpg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
The iron ore market saw narrow fluctuations today, with the DCE main contract I2609 eventually closing at 759.5 RMB/ton,unchanged from the previous day. Spot prices at Qingdao Port fell by 2-5 RMB/ton compared to the previous trading day. Traders sold goods in line with market conditions, while steel mills showed improved purchasing willingness, and market trading activity was better than yesterday. Among them, the transaction price for PB fines at Rizhao Port was 708 RMB/ton. According to SMM survey data, as of July 16, the total inventory across the 10 ports monitored by SMM stood at 106.27 million tonnes, down 1.87 million tonnes from the previous period. Inventories of fines, concentrates, and pellets declined, while lump ore saw a slight accumulation. This indicates that overall iron ore demand remains resilient. Although market chatter suggests that environmental protection production restrictions have begun in Tangshan, they have not yet affected hot metal output. The iron ore fundamentals remain relatively stable. However, given expectations of reduced supply of Ukrainian concentrates due to the Russia-Ukraine conflict, and with the upcoming July Politburo meeting fueling strong market expectations for policy support in the second half of the year, a short-term price rebound is anticipated.

Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
The iron ore market traded sideways today, with the DCE main contract I2609 eventually closing at 762 RMB/ton, up 0.53% from the previous day'sclosing price. Spot prices mostly followed with gains of 0-2 RMB/ton. Traders sold goods in line with market conditions; as the weekend approached, steel mills showed weaker purchasing willingness, and market trading activity was generally moderate.As of July 17, the latest SMM statistics show that iron ore inventory across 35 major ports nationwide totaled 145.13 million tonnes, down 2.03 million tonnes from the previous period, marking another round of destocking. During the same period, the average daily port oŏake fell slightly by 55,000 tonnes to 3.22 million tonnes. This week, due to short-term weather disruptions, arrivals at some ports dropped notably, and resource replenishment tightened significantly. Looking ahead to next week, although iron ore demand is expected to weaken further, the US-Iran conflict and longterm contract negotiations will continue to create disturbances in the market. Prices are expected to maintain a range-bound fluctuation next week.
Linked asset: 17072026chinese.jpg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today, the DCE iron ore futures moved sideways today, with the most-traded DCE I2609 contract closing at 758 yuan/mt, edging down 0.39%from yesterday's closing price. Spot prices fell by 0-5 yuan/mt in tandem; traders sold based on market conditions; steel mills purchased as needed, and overall market trading sentiment on Monday was subdued. Supply side, total global iron ore shipments tracked by SMM last week reached 30.98 million mt, up 2% WoW, with cumulative growth of 1% YoY. Shipments from Australia and Brazil both edged up, but those from non-mainstream countries dropped notably WoW, while the four major miners posted a slight increase. Meanwhile, total iron ore arrivals at Chinese ports last week came to 19.75 million mt, down 21% WoW but with cumulative growth of 4% YoY, mainly affected by typhoons and heavy rains; a recovery is expected this week. Demand side, steel mill shipments fell further due to the weather, weakening their willingness to purchase raw materials and in turn weighing on spot prices. Overall, ore prices are expected to continue their sideways consolidation in the short term
Linked asset: 20072026chines23.jpg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today, the DCE iron ore futures trended weakly, with a slight recovery in the afternoon. The most-traded DCE I2609 contract closed at 749 yuan/mt,up 1.13%. Spot prices at Qingdao Port fell about 5-12 yuan/mt from the previous trading day. Trader quoting enthusiasm was mediocre, and steel mill purchase willingness was mediocre. As of now, spot cargo trading volumes were mediocre. Currently, supply-side pressure in iron ore has not yet become prominent, and overall inventory remains in a destocking trend. As of last weekend, total iron ore inventory across 35 main ports nationwide stood at 145.13 million mt, down 2.03 million mt WoW; daily average port pick-up volume edged down 55,000 mt to 3.22 million mt. Meanwhile, blast furnace maintenance intensity increased further. According to SMM statistics, the hot metal output impact from blast furnace maintenance this week was 1.3584 million mt, up 67,500 mt WoW. Next week, the hot metal impact is expected to be 1.5097 million mt, up 151,300 mt from this week, and the demand side is therefore expected to continue to decline. Looking at the overall supply-demand fundamentals, iron ore prices are expected to consolidate on a subdued note in the short term
Linked asset: 21072026chinese23.jpg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

IRON ORE SEABORNE INDEX (IOSI) MARKET COMMENTARY
Jul 21st, 2026 CFR Qingdao, USD/dry tonne Today, the DCE iron ore futures trended weakly, with a slight recovery in the a(cid:332)ernoon. The most-traded DCE I2609 contract closed at 749 yuan/mt,
up 1.13%. Spot prices at Qingdao Port fell about 5-12 yuan/mt from the previous trading day. Trader quo(cid:415)ng enthusiasm was mediocre, and steel mill
purchase willingness was mediocre. As of now, spot cargo trading volumes were mediocre.

text:
Today, the DCE iron ore futures weakened today. The most-traded DCE i2609 contract closed at 739.5 yuan/mt, down 1.00%. The spot price at Qingdao Portfell around 4-8 yuan/mt from the previous trading day. Traders showed moderate enthusiasm in offering prices, while steel mills remained largely on the sidelines. As of now, spot trading volumes were moderate. According to this week's SMM survey, the capacity utilization rate of blast furnaces at sampled steel mills was 89.49%, down 0.50 percentage point WoW. Daily average hot metal production at sampled steel mills was 2.4247 million mt, down 13,500 mt WoW. Although this round of environmental protection-driven production restrictions was weaker than expected, hot metal production is expected to continue its downward trend. Meanwhile, environmental and safety inspection factors have not yet dissipated, and some steel mills may have temporary maintenance plans. Overall, blast furnace hot metal production is likely to keep declining in the short term, and iron ore demand is expected to remain weak. Therefore, iron ore prices are expected to consolidate with a bearish bias in the short term.
Linked asset: 22072026chines.jpg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)

text:
Today, the DCE iron ore futures moved strongly today. The most-traded DCE I2609 contract closed at 747.5 yuan/mt, up 0.74%. Spot prices at Qingdao Portrose roughly 5-8 yuan/mt from the previous trading day. Trader activity was moderate, and inquiries from steel mills were limited. Spot trading was poor. Recently, steel mills' demand for iron ore has slowed. According to SMM data, inventories at the ten major ports totaled 105.63 million mt, down only 640,000 mt WoW. Based on current inventory structure, concentrate and lump ore destocking was relatively large, pellet inventories were at low levels, while fines saw a slight buildup. Demand side, off-season conditions persisted. Although environmental protection-driven production restrictions were relatively modest, the likelihood of steel mills voluntarily conducting blast furnace maintenance still exists. Next week's Politburo meeting expectations are heating up, potentially boosting market sentiment in the short term. On the whole, however, iron ore prices are expected to continue to move sideways in the near term.
Linked asset: 23072026chinese.jpg
Source: Metals Market Index (MMI)
